assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence:
review-30001.md contains '## Classification: code change request' and '## Action

Sub-task creation required.' A corresponding subtask-30001.md file was created. The report.md confirms: 'Comment 30001 -- code change request' and 'Action: Sub-task created to wrap the operations in self.db.transaction()'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains '## Classification: suggestion' and explains the reasoning: '"should also add" -- while "should" can be directive, the addition of "also" softens it to a suggestion', '"would help" -- conditional language indicating the reviewer believes this is beneficial but not strictly necessary', '"Something like:" -- explicitly frames the provided SQL as an example approach, not a mandated implementation'. All three suggestive language markers ('should also', 'would help', 'Something like') are explicitly analyzed.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence:
review-30003.md contains '## Classification: nit' and '## Action

No sub-task created. Nit-class feedback does not trigger sub-task creation.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory. Report.md confirms: 'Comment 30003 -- nit' and 'Action: No sub-task created.'

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence:
review-30004.md contains '## Classification: question' and '## Action

No sub-task created. Question-class feedback requires a response from the PR author, not a tracked code change.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory. Report.md confirms: 'Comment 30004 -- question' and 'Action: No sub-task created.'

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md (the only sub-task) contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1: 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 7: wrapping UPDATE statements in transaction), '## Files to Modify' (line 10: 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs'), '## Implementation Notes' (line 13: SeaORM transaction API details), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 20: 4 acceptance criteria checkboxes).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section (line 31) with the exact URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 32).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section (line 34) with 'Comment ID: 30001', 'Author: reviewer-a', 'File: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, line 60', and 'Original comment:' followed by the full review comment text: 'The soft_delete method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification/analysis files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), one sub-task description (subtask-30001.md), and the verification report (report.md). No code files (.rs, .toml, .sql, etc.) exist in the outputs directory. The report makes no mention of code modifications. The sub-task file only describes what should be done, it does not contain code changes.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have individual classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), and the report.md's 'Review Feedback' row lists all four: '1 code change request (comment 30001: transaction wrapping), 1 suggestion (comment 30002: index addition), 1 nit (comment 30003: context message), 1 question (comment 30004: GET behavior)'. The 'Review Feedback Details' section covers all four comments with summaries and actions.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md contains no merge commands, no 'gh pr merge' invocations, no merge status mentions, and no auto-merge references. The overall status is 'WARN' (not merged), and the report explicitly states a sub-task was created for the code change request, indicating the PR requires further work before merging.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file (listed under Files to Create in task spec). 5 new test functions added, 0 removed, 0 modified. No existing test files were changed. |' The detailed section also states: 'tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file (does not exist on the base branch). All 5 test functions are additions. No existing test files were modified or deleted. Classification: ADDITIVE.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a full '### Convention Upgrade Eligibility Analysis' section that evaluates: (1) CONVENTIONS.md check -- 'no CONVENTIONS.md content is available in the fixture data for analysis', (2) Codebase pattern check -- 'a codebase pattern cannot be established from the available fixture data', and (3) Upgrade decision -- 'the suggestion is NOT upgraded. General industry best practices... are explicitly excluded as upgrade evidence'. The report.md also confirms in 'Style/Conventions Analysis': 'One suggestion examined (comment 30002: partial index on deleted_at). Not upgraded -- no CONVENTIONS.md content available to verify index creation conventions, and no codebase pattern demonstrable from the available fixture data.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classification is 'suggestion' with action 'No sub-task created. The classification remains "suggestion" -- the reviewer proposes an optional performance enhancement using suggestive language, and no project convention backing was found to warrant an upgrade.' No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. The report confirms: 'Comment 30002 -- suggestion' with 'Action: No sub-task created.'

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence:
report.md Test Quality row states: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews found in PR.' The detailed section elaborates: 'Eval Quality -- N/A
No eval result reviews found in the PR. No github-actions[bot] reviews with ## Eval Results marker and sdlc-workflow/run-evals footer detected.' This confirms the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) was applied and found no matches, resulting in N/A. The overall Test Quality is PASS without Eval Quality affecting the combination.
